sections.paragraph:
ἑβδομάς, άδος, ἡ week (Hippocr., Aphorism. 2, 24; Lydus, Mens. 2, 4 p. 21 W et al.; PPrinc III, 179, 16 [V–VI a.d.]; LXX; ApcEsdr, Philo; TestLevi 16:1; 17:10.—In Jos.=sabbath: Bell. 2, 147 and 4, 99, C. Ap. 2, 175) B 16:6 (quot. of uncertain origin; s. Windisch, Hdb. ad loc.).—B. 1005. DELG s.v. ἑπτά. TW.

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Lydus Lydus = Joannes Laurentius Lydus, VI a.d.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PPrinc PPrinc = Papyri in the Princeton University Collections—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

ApcEsdr ApcEsdr = Apocalypse of Esdras—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

TestLevi TestLevi = Testament of Levi, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

quot. quot. = quotation

Hdb. Hdb. = Handbuch (in connection with a specific NT book, the ref. is to a commentary in the series Handbuch zum Neuen Testament, founded by HLietzmann)—List 6

ad loc. ad loc. = ad locum (to or at the place under consideration)

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 269
